name: monastery-refectory
summary: The communal dining hall of a monastery — long tables, benches, a lectern for the day's reading. Meals are a sacred act here; silence and shared bread under a single holy image.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: long — one or two great tables running the length of the room

accent.purpose: a head-of-room dais where the abbot's seat and lectern stand

primary_palette.note: a single long refectory table with benches along both sides
two parallel tables with a central walk between
focus on the reader's lectern; the tables fall into place around it
a great hearth at one end, with the head table close and the rest ranged beyond

story_prompts: a meal in silence — brothers eating, the reader halfway through a passage at the lectern
between services — tables set, food not yet brought, a single novice lighting candles
a feast-day exception — wine on the tables, ornaments out, the usual silence broken
a meal interrupted — half-eaten bowls, benches pushed back, everyone rushed out
the monastery has fallen — long-cold hearth, mice in the bread, one last brother at the lectern dead of plague

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: warm and practical (0.4-0.5) — hearth at one end, candles along the tables, the lectern well-lit

anti_patterns: tables in rigorous straight lines — this is a monastic space, not a tavern
the lectern has a clear sightline to every brother on the benches — never obstruct it
avoid throne-scale chairs for any seat — even the abbot's chair is plain
